title: diasperus subcarnosus
intro: Diasperus subcarnosus, also known as Mexican prickly poppy or Mexican poppy, is a flowering plant native to Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Diasperus subcarnosus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Diasperus subcarnosus is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
